metadata.legal_basis: Contract formation requires offer, acceptance, and consideration; acceptance by performance applies to unilateral contracts.

text: Identifies the father's conditional promise and the son's marriage within 18 months as offer and acceptance forming a unilateral contract.

metadata.legal_basis: Consideration requires a bargained-for exchange; marriage as requested performance may or may not be consideration depending on inducement.

text: Recognizes the issue whether the son's marriage constitutes bargained-for consideration or is merely a condition on a gratuitous promise.

metadata.legal_basis: Absence of signed writing bars enforcement of contracts in consideration of marriage under the Statute of Frauds.

text: Explains that no signed writing exists between father and son, making the oral promise unenforceable under the marriage-consideration Statute of Frauds.

metadata.legal_basis: Suretyship writing requirement applies to promises made to creditor to answer for another's debt; promise to debtor generally not covered.

text: Analyzes the suretyship Statute of Frauds provision and concludes it likely does not apply because the promise was made to the son (debtor), not the creditor.

metadata.legal_basis: Promissory estoppel requires inducement and reliance; courts are cautious applying it to Statute of Frauds categories.

text: Evaluates promissory estoppel as a possible but weak exception to the Statute of Frauds due to weak inducement and reliance by the son.

metadata.legal_basis: Prior intent to perform requested act undermines inducement element of consideration and estoppel.

text: Notes that the son's prior intent to marry the politician's daughter weakens inducement and reliance for consideration and promissory estoppel.

metadata.legal_basis: Unilateral mistake about expected benefit generally does not excuse performance unless promise conditioned on that benefit.

text: States that the father's unilateral mistake about the availability of a tax deduction does not excuse performance or affect enforceability absent express condition.

metadata.legal_basis: Statute of Frauds bars enforcement of oral contracts made in consideration of marriage without signed writing.

text: Concludes that the father's oral promise is unenforceable due to the marriage-consideration Statute of Frauds writing requirement and absence of writing.

metadata.legal_basis: One-year provision is distinct and does not apply if performance possible within one year; marriage-consideration writing requirement is separate.

text: Avoids material doctrinal error by not conflating the one-year provision with the marriage-consideration Statute of Frauds requirement.

metadata.legal_basis: Acknowledging counterarguments demonstrates comprehensive legal reasoning and issue spotting.

text: Includes counterarguments such as the son's prior intent negating inducement and the possibility of promissory estoppel.
The response identifies the controlling legal issue raised by the facts.
The response addresses the principal counterargument or defense when relevant.

metadata.repair_reason: No final rubric directly covers the extracted element.

text: The response addresses the element of Main purpose of promisor.
